symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic recurrent perineal / suprapubic / low-back pain for 8+ months
symptoms.character: deep aching, 'golf ball of crushed glass' perineal sensation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: chronic pelvic pain syndrome (chronic prostatitis, category III)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic relapsing perineal/pelvic pain >3 months is the defining feature of CP/CPPS.
A chronic relapsing course over months is against an acute bacterial infection, which is an acute febrile illness.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: chronic pelvic pain syndrome (chronic prostatitis, category III)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Post-ejaculatory pain is a characteristic feature of chronic prostatitis / CPPS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Irritative voiding — dysuria, frequency and urgency

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: chronic pelvic pain syndrome (chronic prostatitis, category III)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Irritative voiding symptoms accompany CP/CPPS flares in the absence of infection.
Dysuria/frequency/urgency also fit a UTI — the reason urinalysis is required to discriminate, which cannot be done remotely.
Irritative voiding can accompany acute prostatitis; discriminated by fever and prostate examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Flare triggered by acute psychological stress

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: chronic pelvic pain syndrome (chronic prostatitis, category III)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Psychological stress is a recognised precipitant of CP/CPPS flares via pelvic floor overactivity and central sensitisation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Multiple prior antibiotic courses with no benefit; prior clear urine tests

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: chronic pelvic pain syndrome (chronic prostatitis, category III)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Failure to respond to repeated antibiotics with previously sterile urine is characteristic of non-bacterial CP/CPPS and argues against a treatable bacterial infection.
Repeatedly sterile urine and antibiotic non-response argue against a bacterial cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever, rigors and flank pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute bacterial prostatitis is typically a febrile, toxic illness; being afebrile with no rigors makes it much less likely.
No fever, rigors or flank pain argues strongly against upper-tract infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of visible haematuria and preserved ability to void

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No visible haematuria and no colicky flank pain argue against a stone.
Preserved voiding argues against acute retention as a complication.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal, non-boggy prostate on digital rectal examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A tender, boggy, swollen prostate would indicate acute prostatitis; a normal non-boggy prostate argues against it. Clinician DRE finding — not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Exquisitely tender pelvic floor musculature (levator/perineal) on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: chronic pelvic pain syndrome (chronic prostatitis, category III)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pelvic floor myalgia with exquisitely tender levator musculature (rather than a tender prostate) localises the pain to the pelvic floor and supports CP/CPPS. Clinician palpation finding — not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Point-of-care urinalysis negative for nitrites, leukocytes and blood

investigation_details.expected_result: Negative nitrites, negative leukocyte esterase, negative blood
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without urinalysis a urinary/prostatic infection cannot be excluded, and CP/CPPS cannot be safely diagnosed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clean dipstick argues strongly against active bacterial UTI. Clinician point-of-care test — not obtainable via telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Expressed prostatic secretion / urine culture (expected negative)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Expressed prostatic secretion microscopy and culture / midstream urine culture
investigation_details.expected_result: No bacterial growth (sterile) — supports non-bacterial CP/CPPS
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Confirmation of the non-bacterial nature relies on culture; without it the category (III vs II) cannot be established.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: chronic pelvic pain syndrome (chronic prostatitis, category III)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sterile cultures define the non-bacterial category III CP/CPPS — laboratory investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Chronic pelvic pain syndrome flare pattern
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: chronic pelvic pain syndrome (chronic prostatitis, category III)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Chronic relapsing perineal pain + post-ejaculatory pain + stress trigger + antibiotic non-response + tender pelvic floor is the CP/CPPS constellation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Discriminates a normal non-boggy prostate (CP/CPPS) from a tender boggy prostate (acute bacterial prostatitis); requires in-person same-day assessment.
First-line bedside test to exclude active urinary infection; requires in-person same-day assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Midstream urine microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the absence of a bacterial cause; supports the non-bacterial CP/CPPS category.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical repeat antibiotic course without testing
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The patient has already failed multiple antibiotic courses with prior sterile urine; repeating empirical antibiotics without confirming infection is inappropriate and is a key error to avoid.
